deploy-dev.yml: 3 third-party actions not pinned to SHA — vulnerable to supply chain attacks
deploy-prod.yml: 3 third-party actions not pinned to SHA — vulnerable to supply chain attacks
deploy-prod.yml: no explicit permissions block — defaults to read-write (overly permissive)
discord-notification.yml: CI install without frozen lockfile — builds may not be reproducible
seed-dev-account.yml: no explicit permissions block — defaults to read-write (overly permissive)
seed-dev-account.yml: CI install without frozen lockfile — builds may not be reproducible
package.json missing repository field — provenance attestation won't link to source
No SECURITY.md — users don't know how to report vulnerabilities
No CODEOWNERS file — no mandatory reviewers for critical paths
No CONTRIBUTING.md — onboarding is harder for new contributors
No pre-commit hooks (husky/lefthook) — lint/format not enforced before commit
No Dependabot/Renovate — dependency updates are manual and often forgotten
No formatter config (Prettier/Biome/.editorconfig) — inconsistent code formatting
TypeScript strict mode not enabled — allows implicit any and null errors
No commit convention enforcement (commitlint/changesets) — changelog generation is manual
No error tracking (Sentry/Bugsnag) — production errors may go unnoticed
No integration tests — consider testing service boundaries
No E2E test framework (Playwright/Cypress) — critical user flows untested
Only 5% of source files have matching test files
High mock ratio: 1.04 mocks per assertion — tests may not reflect real behavior
No Content-Security-Policy meta tag in HTML

details.description: Detects contradictions between documentation and code. Finds stale README claims, incorrect JSDoc, outdated API docs, and misleading comments.
Detects internal contradictions: inconsistent validation, conflicting defaults, naming drift, dead config flags, and behavioral mismatches across modules.
